text.title: chunking-code-ast Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for chunking-code-ast. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: CodeChunker MUST dispatch on language: python uses ast.parse on the full source and walks FunctionDef/AsyncFunctionDef/ClassDef nodes; javascript/typescript uses regex patterns for function, class, and arrow function definitions. For any language where the primary path fails (SyntaxError for Python, empty match list for JS), CodeChunker MUST fall back to _chunk_generic and MUST log a warning indicating that metadata fields (name, type, docstring) will be absent in the output chunks.
rule.rationale: Silent fallback without a warning causes the caller to index chunks as if they were named function units, but they are actually anonymous line-based fragments. Surfacing the fallback allows the pipeline to flag these files for manual review or exclusion.
